stories.title: Adaptive depth for simple tasks
stories.story: As a developer analyzing a simple change, I want the roundtable to sense my brevity and reduce probing so that I spend minutes, not half an hour, on analysis for straightforward work.

stories.title: Deep engagement on complex tasks
stories.story: As a developer analyzing a complex change, I want the roundtable to sense my engagement and probe deeper so that edge cases and hidden requirements are surfaced before I start building.

stories.title: Transparent assumptions during confirmation
stories.story: As a developer reviewing analysis results, I want to see what the roundtable assumed on my behalf so that I can catch incorrect inferences before they become requirements.

stories.title: Drill into assumption details on demand
stories.story: As a developer who sees a topic-level assumption summary, I want to expand into FR-level detail on demand so that I can review specific inferences without being forced to read everything.

stories.title: Scope recommendation from conversation
stories.story: As a developer who has just completed analysis, I want the roundtable to recommend the appropriate build scope based on what it learned so that I don't need to decide upfront whether to pass --light.

stories.title: No need to know about --light flag
stories.story: As a developer who has never heard of --light, I want the framework to figure out scope automatically so that I get the right level of analysis without learning framework flags.

stories.title: Mixed depth across topics
stories.story: As a developer who cares deeply about one aspect of a change but not others, I want the roundtable to go deep where I engage and brief where I don't so that my time is spent where it matters.
